summary: Concept and synthesis quality review initially found English-heavy template prose, weak concept follow-through, and new concept pages that only pointed at same-day intake sources. The live corpus now carries mature Korean-first analysis pages, integrated concept bridge prose, and a reusable raw-intake promotion profile runtime that scaffolds, validates, and renders mature synthesis pages plus explicit concept carryover decisions.

observations.problem: The promotion pass could create structurally valid synthesis pages while still leaving English-heavy template prose, analysis sections that read like routing memos, and source pages that linked to syntheses without also exposing the canonical concept layer.
observations.why_it_matters: A route can look complete in lint and stage2 while still feeling unfinished to readers if the prose remains mixed-language or if a concept-worthy family is left without canonical linkage.
observations.automation_candidate: Use a promotion-profile runtime as the primary fix: scaffold new families from the absorption matrix, require reviewed mature analysis text plus explicit concept carryover decisions, then render synthesis/concept pages from that manifest. Keep lint review candidates for promotion-memo analysis markers and synthesis-linked source pages missing concept links as the closeout backstop.
observations.suggested_followup: Updated on 2026-04-22 KST: the promotion runtime now renders both new-family and refresh-existing synthesis pages from reviewed manifests, requires bridge-source interpretation to be integrated into the normal concept body, and leaves `synthesis_analysis_template_drift_candidate`, `synthesis_follow_up_split_candidate`, `concept_carryover_continuity_missing_candidate`, and `concept_carryover_split_candidate` as review backstops.

observations.problem: New concept pages created from raw intake were initially anchored only on same-day source pages, while older concepts did not automatically absorb later follow-up raw that clearly belonged to their canonical scope.
observations.why_it_matters: Concept pages then read like batch-local artifacts instead of cross-batch canonical interpretation anchors, and readers had to jump through syntheses to see temporal continuity.
observations.automation_candidate: Require every promoted concept family to declare `focus_source_stems`, `bridge_source_stems`, and a non-empty `carryover_decision` in the promotion manifest, then render concept pages from that reviewed profile instead of from source-only intake defaults. Reject split `continuity_blocks` and use source-registration bridge suggestions so the bridge review starts before promotion.
observations.suggested_followup: Updated on 2026-04-22 KST: promoted concept families now require `focus_source_stems`, `bridge_source_stems`, and a non-empty `carryover_decision`; `continuity_blocks` are rejected; live concept pages were re-rendered/backfilled with bridge prose merged into `Main body`; and `source_slug_curation scaffold` now proposes `family_bridge_candidates` during source registration.
